stemHtml: The correct definition of a safe forced landing is…

options.a: a voluntary landing on land or sea carried out by the crew in order to protect the aircraft and its occupants.
options.b: a landing on land or sea from which it is guaranteed no injuries will result to the occupants.
options.c: an unavoidable landing or ditching with a reasonable expectancy of no injuries to persons in the aircraft or on the surface.
options.d: an inevitable landing on land or sea from which one may reasonably expect no injuries on board.
